content:
# Gravity_Survey

**Summary**: A geophysical method measuring variations in Earth's gravity to map subsurface density differences.

---

## How It Is Used in the Thesis Collection

- [[2024-ahmed|Ahmed, 2024]] applied gravity surveys to the Al-Hassa National Park study to identify complex subsurface lithology and tectonic settings.

- [[2024-chavanidis|Chavanidis, 2024]] used gravity survey to model 3D subsurface structures and identify potential geothermal reservoirs.

## Instruments and Software



- [[2024-chavanidis|Chavanidis, 2024]] used gravity survey to model 3D subsurface structures and identify potential geothermal reservoirs.

## Typical Workflow



## Key Results and Findings

- The residual gravity field showed low-gravity areas (blue) in western/southern parts and high values (red/pink) in northern/southeastern parts, with a total amplitude of 2.44 mGal. ([[2024-ahmed|Ahmed, 2024, p. 48]])

- The 3D gravity model identified bodies with both positive and negative density contrasts (e.g., Line A and Line B). ([[2024-chavanidis|Chavanidis, 2024, p. 46]])
